commentaries.author: Hippolytus of Rome
commentaries.content: But just the Word? And what is it that is begotten of Him, but just the Spirit,

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: The scribes asserted that only God could forgive sins, yet Jesus not only forgave sins, but showed that he had also another power that belongs to God alone: the power to disclose the secrets of the heart. The Gospel of St. Matthew, Homily

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content:
Of our heretic. Now, that the very Lord Himself of all might, the Word and Spirit of the Father.
But if repentance is a thing human, its baptism must necessarily be of the same nature: else, if it had been celestial, it would have given both the Holy Spirit and remission of sins. But none either pardons sins or freely grants the Spirit save God only.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
